items.q: Where does 'petit' usually go in relation to its noun?
items.misconception: After the noun like most French adjectives.
items.random_wrong: Either position with the same meaning.
items.why: Students apply 'all French adjectives follow nouns' too rigidly. BAGS adjectives (Beauty, Age, Goodness, Size) typically PRECEDE. Petit (size) and bon (goodness) come before.

items.q: How do you say 'I do not eat' in French?
items.why: Students forget the verb 'sandwich'. Ne and pas wrap AROUND the verb in present tense - ne BEFORE the verb, pas AFTER. In compound tenses, they wrap around the auxiliary.

items.correct: A verb in the subjunctive mood.
items.why: Students use infinitive after impersonal expressions. 'Il faut que tu PARTES' (subjunctive), not 'il faut que tu partir'. The que triggers subjunctive in the following clause.

items.q: Who won the Nobel Prize for Literature in 2022?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Annie Ernaux. “Marguerite Duras” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Martinique. “Senegal” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was L'Amant published?
items.why: The real figure is 1984. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
